---
title: "OpenClaw Memory Index"
format: gfm
---

# OpenClaw Memory Index

This file is a durable, human-readable memory index in QMD format.
It exists to make future recall easier by giving important operating context a stable home outside of raw daily notes.

## How to use this file
- Put durable patterns here, not every transient event.
- Keep daily chronology in `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`.
- Use this file for:
  - stable operating rules
  - long-lived workflow expectations
  - room/agent ownership
  - recurring decisions worth remembering

## Durable workflow memory

### Engineering and QA
- Engineering is the live implementation room.
- Engineering can look at other rooms for context when asked to do work originating there.
- Engineering work is not considered complete just because coding finished.
- Default completion loop:

  Engineering -> QA -> Engineering (if needed) -> QA -> Done/Approval

- QA is the review gate for code, config, automation, integration, and other engineering-impact work.

### Room ownership
- `engineering` -> `engineering`
- `signal-and-circuit` -> `signal-and-circuit`
- `command-center` -> `command-center`
- `ops-desk` -> `ops-desk`
- `support-inbox` -> `support-inbox`
- `caruso-growth` -> `caruso-growth`
- `caruso-product` -> `caruso-product`
- `security-infra` -> `security-infra`
- `research-lab` -> `research-lab`
- `game-dev` -> `game-dev`
- `qa-review` -> `qa-review` role/function

### Canonical reference docs
- `ROOMS_AND_WORKFLOW_SPEC.md`
- `ENGINEERING_ROOM_GUIDE.md`
- `ENGINEERING_QA_LOOP.md`
- `GAME_PROJECT_OPERATING_SPEC.md`

### Game studio memory
- Initial game project room: `game-dev`
- Initial game coordination role: `Chief of Staff`
- Chief of Staff is the studio-operations coordination layer, responsible for synthesis, alignment, prioritization, and conflict escalation without replacing specialist roles.
- Initial creative leadership role: `Game Director`
- Game Director protects the game’s vision, fun, cohesion, and player experience, and should challenge feature creep or weak-fit mechanics.
- Initial production leadership role: `Executive Producer`
- Executive Producer protects milestone realism, scope containment, delivery pace, and ship viability without sacrificing game identity for arbitrary speed.
- Initial engineering leadership role: `Technical Director`
- Technical Director protects architecture, maintainability, stability, performance, and technical debt control as the game grows.
- Initial visual leadership role: `Art Director`
- Art Director protects visual identity, readability, tonal consistency, and overall style cohesion.
- Initial narrative leadership role: `Narrative Director`
- Narrative Director protects story logic, lore consistency, dialogue quality, tone, and emotional coherence with gameplay.
- Initial audio leadership role: `Audio Director`
- Audio Director protects sonic identity, gameplay feedback clarity, emotional tone, and cohesive sound language.
- Initial systems design role: `Gameplay Systems Designer`
- Gameplay Systems Designer protects system depth, clarity, balance integrity, meaningful player choices, and replayable reward loops.
- Initial gameplay AI role: `AI Systems Designer`
- AI Systems Designer protects readable challenge, fairness, behavior variety, and scalable AI encounter quality.
- Initial progression/economy role: `Economy Designer`
- Economy Designer protects resource health, reward pacing, progression fairness, sink/faucet stability, and meaningful long-term value loops.
- Initial user experience role: `UX Director`
- UX Director protects usability, readability, accessibility, onboarding clarity, input confidence, and information hierarchy.
- Initial player research simulation role: `Player Experience Analyst`
- Player Experience Analyst simulates likely player frustration, delight, confusion, and drop-off risk so design intent stays grounded in player reality.
- Initial QA leadership role: `Head of QA`
- Head of QA protects test strategy, coverage quality, regression awareness, release confidence, and evidence-based quality risk reporting.
- Initial quality engineering role: `QA Automation Engineer`
- QA Automation Engineer protects automation coverage, repeatability, regression speed, build verification, and stable test infrastructure.
- Initial adversarial QA role: `Exploit Tester`
- Exploit Tester protects system integrity, fairness, progression pacing, and economy stability against hostile or degenerate player behavior.
- Initial product strategy role: `Product Manager`
- Product Manager protects audience fit, roadmap value, product clarity, and market-facing prioritization without collapsing into trend-chasing.
- Initial growth/community role: `Marketing and Community Director`
- Marketing and Community Director protects player trust, external messaging clarity, brand voice, and honest audience-facing positioning.
- Initial analytics role: `Data Analyst`
- Data Analyst protects evidence quality, useful metric interpretation, honest uncertainty, and actionability over vanity metrics.
- Initial build/release role: `Build and Release Manager`
- Build and Release Manager protects build stability, release predictability, version integrity, packaging quality, and deployment repeatability.

## Updating guidance
Update this file when:
- room ownership changes
- the engineering/QA workflow changes
- a new durable operating rule is adopted
- an implementation pattern becomes the normal expected workflow

## Memory writing convention

### Put in daily notes (`memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`)
Use daily notes for:
- chronological events
- one-off debugging findings
- what changed today
- temporary observations
- partial progress notes
- short-lived blockers

### Put in QMD memory (`memory/MEMORY_INDEX.qmd`)
Use QMD memory for:
- durable workflow rules
- room/agent ownership that should persist
- recurring operating expectations
- stable handoff rules
- long-lived decisions worth recalling later

### Rule of thumb
If it matters mainly because it happened today, put it in the daily note.
If it should still matter weeks from now, summarize it here.
